originalTitle: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
title: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
content: Ismail Omar Guelleh, 78, has extended his already 27-year rule in a poll boycotted by the main opposition candidates.

originalTitle: Nearly 400 sentenced in Nigeria for links to militant Islamists
title: Big News from Nigeria: Judges Help Keep Everyone Safe!
content:
Hello, wonderful friends across our amazing continent of Africa! Your friendly kid reporter is here to share some important news with you today. We're going to talk about a big country called Nigeria and how grown-ups are working hard to keep everyone safe and happy.

Meet Nigeria: A Land of Many Smiles!

Nigeria is a very, very big country in West Africa. It has so many people, like a giant playground full of friends! People in Nigeria speak many different languages and have beautiful traditions, music, and delicious food. Kids in Nigeria love to play, learn, and grow, just like you! Did you know? Nigeria has the most people of any country in Africa! Imagine all those amazing smiles!

What's This Big News All About?

Sometimes, grown-ups called 'judges' have to make sure everyone follows the rules. They are like very wise teachers or referees for grown-ups! In Nigeria, these judges have been very busy helping to make things right. They had to talk to many, many people – nearly 400 of them! These people had done things that were not good for the community. They had caused trouble and made some people feel scared. But now, the judges have listened carefully and decided what needs to happen next.

Why Did Some People Cause Trouble?

It's sad to say, but sometimes there are groups of people who don't want peace. They try to make others feel afraid or stop kids from going to school. They might try to cause problems in towns and villages. This is not kind, and it's not fair to anyone. Everyone deserves to live in peace, go to school, and feel safe in their homes. When people cause trouble, it makes it hard for everyone to be happy and for communities to grow stronger.

Who Are the Judges and What Do They Do?

Imagine a referee at a football game. They listen, they watch, and they make sure everyone plays fair. Judges are a bit like that for grown-ups. When something goes wrong, like people causing trouble, the judges listen to all the stories and look at all the facts. This is called a 'trial.' During a trial, they try to understand exactly what happened. They want to be fair to everyone, but they also need to make sure that people who did bad things understand their mistakes. Did you know? In many countries, judges wear special robes to show they are doing very important work to keep communities fair and safe!

What Does 'Sentenced' Mean?

After a judge listens to everything in a trial, they make a decision. This decision is called a 'sentence.' A sentence tells people what they need to do to make things right, or how long they need to think about their actions. For nearly 400 people in Nigeria, the judges said they would spend time, some for five years, and some for a very, very long time, in a place where they can think about the trouble they caused. This is like a very long 'time-out' for grown-ups who did something very wrong. This helps them understand that causing trouble hurts others. It also helps to keep everyone else safe outside.

Keeping Our Communities Safe and Sound!

This news is important because it shows that grown-ups are working hard to protect everyone in Nigeria. When people who cause trouble are held responsible, it helps make sure that kids can go to school, families can work, and everyone can play and live without fear. Our communities need peace and safety to grow. When there is peace, kids can learn new things, artists can create beautiful art, and farmers can grow yummy food for everyone. Did you know? Police officers and other community helpers work every day to keep us safe, just like our parents and teachers do!

Working Together for a Peaceful Africa

This story from Nigeria reminds us that working together is super important. When people choose kindness and peace, amazing things can happen. It means kids can learn and dream big dreams. Every country in Africa wants its people to live in peace and harmony. This news from Nigeria is a step towards making sure that happens for all the wonderful children and families there. Did you know? Many schools in Africa teach children about peace and how to be good friends and neighbours!

Why This Matters For You, Our Little Explorers!

This news means that even when there's trouble, there are good grown-ups, like judges, who work to make things better. It means your safety is important, and people are always trying to make our African communities the best places to live, learn, and play. So, let's all remember to be kind, share, and help each other, just like the good people in Nigeria are working to do! That's all for today from your friendly reporter. Stay curious and stay safe, my friends!
summary: In Nigeria, judges have made important decisions for nearly 400 people who caused trouble, giving them time to think about their actions to help keep communities safe and peaceful.
whyMatters: This news is important because it shows that grown-ups are working hard to make sure everyone in Nigeria can live safely, go to school, and play without being scared, which helps make communities stronger and happier for all kids.

originalTitle: Nearly 400 sentenced in Nigeria for links to militant Islamists
title: Judges Help Keep Everyone Safe in Nigeria!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends across Africa! I'm your friendly news reporter, and I have some important news to share from our big continent. Today, we're talking about Nigeria, a really important country in West Africa. It's a place with so many friendly people and exciting things happening every day!

Big News from Nigeria!

Something important happened in Nigeria recently. Our grown-up leaders and special people called judges are working hard to make sure everyone is safe and happy. Sometimes, there are groups of people who cause trouble. They don't want peace, and they might try to hurt others or stop children from going to school. This is not good, is it? Well, good news! The judges have taken a big step to help keep everyone safe. They looked at a lot of people who were part of these groups that cause trouble. And they decided that these people need to stop causing problems. This is a big step towards more peace and happiness for everyone in Nigeria.

Who are These Judges?

Do you know what a judge is? Imagine a very wise grown-up who sits in a special room called a court. They wear a special robe, sometimes black, to show how important their job is. A judge's job is to listen very carefully to everyone. They listen to what happened and what people did. Then, they decide what is fair and right. It's like when your parents help you decide what's fair if you and your brother or sister have a disagreement. Judges do this for the whole country! Did you know? Judges are like super problem-solvers for big grown-up problems! They make sure rules are followed and that everyone gets treated fairly.

What Happened in Court?

In Nigeria, many people were part of groups that did not want peace. These groups were trying to make trouble and sometimes even hurt people. This made many families sad and worried. So, the judges had a very important job to do. They gathered all the facts and listened carefully. They wanted to find out who was part of these troublemaking groups. It was like a very, very big school assembly, but much more serious! Many people were there. The judges carefully decided that almost 400 people were connected to these groups that cause trouble. That's a lot of people, almost like everyone in your school and a few more schools combined!

What Does 'Sentenced' Mean?

When the judge found out that these people were part of the troublemaking groups, they had to tell them what would happen next. This is called 'sentencing'. It means the judge tells you the consequences for your actions. If someone does something bad, like breaking a rule, there are consequences, right? For these people, the consequences were that they would have to stay in a special place for a long time. Some might stay for five years, which is a very long time, like when you grow from Class 2 to Class 7! Others might have to stay for even longer, for their whole grown-up life. This is to make sure they cannot cause trouble anymore and to give them time to think about their actions. Did you know? The number '400' is like having 40 groups of 10 friends each! Imagine all those people!

Why Is This Important for Us?

This news is very important because it means the grown-ups in Nigeria are working hard to keep you and your families safe. When people who cause trouble are stopped, it helps bring peace. It helps children go to school without fear. It helps families feel safe in their homes. And it helps everyone live together happily. It's like when a superhero catches the bad guys to make sure the city is safe. Our judges and leaders are doing their best to be like superheroes for peace in Nigeria! They want all of Nigeria to be a place of joy and kindness, where children can play and learn without worries.

A Little Bit About Nigeria

Nigeria is a truly amazing country! It's the most populated country in all of Africa, with so many wonderful people. People in Nigeria speak many different languages, like Hausa, Yoruba, and Igbo. They have delicious food, beautiful music, and vibrant clothes. Did you know? Nigeria is sometimes called the 'Giant of Africa' because it's so big and has so many people! It also has some incredible animals, like elephants and lions, in its national parks.

What Can Kids Do for Peace?

Even though you are young, you can also help bring peace! How? By being kind to your friends, by sharing your toys, and by listening to your teachers and parents. When you see something that doesn't feel right, always tell a grown-up you trust. They can help make things better. Remember, most people in the world, and especially in Nigeria, want peace and happiness. This news shows that the good people are working very hard to make sure peace wins. Let's all help by being good friends and kind neighbors every single day!
summary: Judges in Nigeria have told almost 400 people who caused trouble that they will have to stay away for a long time, helping to keep everyone safe and bring peace.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows grown-ups are working hard to stop people who cause trouble, so that all children and families in Nigeria can live safely and happily.
